activities_projects.title: Critical Analysis of the Poem
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the poem’s theme, structure, and deeper meaning
steps: Read the poem thoroughly
Analyze the structure and progression of ideas
Write 4–5 sentences explaining how the poet portrays the resilience of nature and the difficulty of destroying it
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops critical interpretation and analytical skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis and textual support (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Figurative Language and Imagery
activities_projects.objective: Identify advanced literary devices and their impact
steps: Find examples of imagery, metaphor, personification, and repetition
Explain how these devices emphasize the tree’s strength and nature’s resilience
Write a paragraph analyzing the effect of these devices on the reader
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes and interprets literary techniques and their effects
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification and insightful explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Philosophical and Environmental Reflection
activities_projects.objective: Explore ethical and philosophical implications of the poem
steps: Reflect on the poem’s message about human interference in nature
Write 3–4 sentences connecting the poem to environmental protection and sustainability
Discuss how patience, care, and understanding of nature are important
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops philosophical thinking and moral reasoning

activities_projects.title: Textual Analysis of Tone and Mood
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the tone, mood, and emotional impact of the poem
steps: Identify lines that express warning, respect, or reflection
Explain how the poet’s tone conveys seriousness and reverence for nature
Write a short paragraph linking tone and mood to the poem’s theme
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Enhances understanding of tone, mood, and poetic effect
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate analysis with textual examples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Cause and Effect Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand the consequences of human actions on nature
steps: Identify lines showing the consequences of simply cutting or hacking the tree
Explain why only removing the root can truly destroy a tree
Write a paragraph connecting the poem to environmental responsibility
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops analytical thinking and moral reasoning
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate cause-effect explanation with textual support (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparative Literature Activity
activities_projects.objective: Compare the poem with other environmental or nature-related literature
steps: Think of another poem, story, or essay that emphasizes respect for nature
Compare the treatment of nature and human responsibility in both works
Write similarities and differences in a table or short paragraph

activities_projects.title: Creative Perspective Writing
steps: Imagine you are the tree being cut
Write a diary entry expressing your feelings about resilience, human interference, and survival
Include reflection on patience, struggle, and growth
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Enhances empathy, perspective-taking, and creative writing skills

activities_projects.title: Advanced Vocabulary and Contextual Usage
activities_projects.objective: Learn and use advanced words from the poem
steps: Find challenging words such as leprous, anchoring, scorching, withering
Explain their contextual meanings in the poem
Write complex sentences using these words in a nature-related context
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct usage and sentence construction (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Creative Story Expansion
steps: Write a short story imagining a world where humans destroy or protect trees
Include consequences, reflection, and ethical reasoning
Use descriptive language and narrative techniques
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Enhances creative and narrative writing skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, structure, and language use (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Discussion and Debate
steps: Debate whether humans respect nature enough and why
Discuss ways to protect the environment inspired by the poem
Write a summary of key points and your personal opinion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops analytical reasoning, communication, and debate skills
